DetectionRule.Name: LSASS Memory Dump Access
DetectionRule.Description: Detects unauthorized access to LSASS process memory, which is used to dump credentials from the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service. Identifies common dumping techniques including MiniDumpWriteDump, Dumpert, and custom memory dumping tools.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1003.001 - OS Credential Dumping: LSASS Memory
DetectionRule.ThreatActors: APT29, APT28, Lazarus, FIN7, Ransomware Groups

RemediationSteps.Step: IMMEDIATE: Isolate affected device from network
Disable compromised user accounts and reset passwords
Check for lateral movement using dumped credentials
Preserve memory dump for forensic analysis
Review security logs for unauthorized access attempts
Implement Credential Guard and LSA Protection

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude Windows Defender and antivirus processes
Exclude legitimate administrative credential audits
Require elevated privileges for alert generation
Time-based: Alert off-hours execution only

DetectionRule.Name: SAM Registry Hive Extraction
DetectionRule.Description: Detects attempts to extract the Security Account Manager (SAM) registry hive, which contains password hashes for local accounts. Monitors registry export operations and shadow copy creation targeting system hives.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1003.002 - OS Credential Dumping: Security Account Manager

RemediationSteps.Step: IMMEDIATE: Check for extracted SAM database files
Reset all local administrator passwords immediately
Disable any accounts that may have been compromised
Check for pass-the-hash attacks using extracted hashes
Enable Credential Guard and LSA Protection
Review who had access to perform registry operations

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude authorized system maintenance windows
Require approval for registry export operations
Exclude backup software during scheduled backups
Time-based: Critical for non-business hours

DetectionRule.Name: Kerberoasting Attack Detection
DetectionRule.Description: Detects Kerberoasting attacks where attackers request service principal names (SPNs) and attempt to crack the associated service account passwords offline. Identifies unusual Kerberos TGS-REQ requests and rapid service ticket requests.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1558.003 - Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets: Kerberoasting
DetectionRule.DetectionLogic:
// Detect via Security Events (Kerberos TGS requests) SecurityEvent | where TimeGenerated > ago(1h) | where EventID == 4769 // Kerberos Service Ticket Request and TicketEncryptionType == 0x17 // RC4 encryption (weak) | extend AccountName = tolower(Account) | summarize TicketRequests = count(), UniqueServices = dcount(ServiceName) by AccountName, IpAddress, Computer | where TicketRequests >= 5 or UniqueServices >= 3 | extend KerberoastRisk = case( TicketRequests >= 10 or UniqueServices >= 5, "Critical", TicketRequests >= 5 or UniqueServices >= 3, "High", "Medium" )
// Also detect via process execution patterns (Rubeus, Kerberoast tools) union ( DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(1h) | where FileName in~ ("Rubeus.exe", "kerberoast", "GetUserSPNs.py", "Invoke-Kerberoast") or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("kerberoast", "GetSPN", "setspn", "-spn") | extend KerberoastTool = case( FileName contains "Rubeus", "Rubeus", FileName contains "python", "Python Kerberoast", FileName contains "PowerShell", "PowerShell", "Unknown Tool" ) | summarize KerberoastAttempts = count() by DeviceId, FileName, AccountName, KerberoastTool | where KerberoastAttempts >= 1 ) | extend FinalRisk = case( KerberoastRisk == "Critical" or KerberoastAttempts >= 3, "Critical", KerberoastRisk == "High" or KerberoastAttempts >= 1, "High", "Medium" ) | where FinalRisk in~ ("Critical", "High") | project-reorder Timestamp, AccountName, FinalRisk, TicketRequests, UniqueServices

RemediationSteps.Step: Reset passwords for all potentially compromised service accounts
Enable AES encryption for Kerberos (disable RC4)
Implement Managed Service Accounts (gMSA) where possible
Review service account password complexity and rotation
Block RC4 encryption in Kerberos policy
Monitor for continued Kerberoasting attempts

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude service accounts from alerting
Exclude authorized security audit activities
Time-based: Focus on off-hours Kerberos activity
Threshold-based: Require minimum 5 ticket requests

DetectionRule.Name: Browser Credential Theft
DetectionRule.Description: Detects unauthorized access to browser password stores and credential harvesting attempts. Monitors access to Chrome, Firefox, Edge password databases and credential dumping tools targeting browser data.
DetectionRule.ThreatActors: Lazarus, APT41, Raccoon Stealer, RedLine Stealer

RemediationSteps.Step: Force password reset for all potentially compromised accounts
Enable multi-factor authentication on all accounts
Remove any saved passwords from affected browsers
Scan device for credential stealing malware
Implement browser isolation or protected mode
Deploy endpoint detection to prevent credential theft

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude legitimate password management tools
Exclude browser backup utilities
Time-based: Alert on non-user-initiated access
Process-based: Validate parent process legitimacy

DetectionRule.Name: ProcDump Credential Access
DetectionRule.Description: Detects misuse of ProcDump or similar memory dumping tools to extract credentials from lsass.exe or other sensitive processes. Identifies command-line indicators of credential dumping disguised as troubleshooting or debugging activities.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1003.001 - OS Credential Dumping: LSASS Memory

RemediationSteps.Step: IMMEDIATE: Check for LSASS memory dump files
Isolate affected device and preserve evidence
Rotate credentials that may have been dumped
Enable LSA Protection and Credential Guard
Block ProcDump on production systems via AppLocker/WDAC
Review why ProcDump was available on the system

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude legitimate debugging and troubleshooting activities
Exclude Microsoft support tools with approved certificates
Time-based: Alert during non-business hours only
User-based: Require elevated privileges

DetectionRule.Name: DPAPI Master Key Extraction Attempt
DetectionRule.Description: Detects attempts to extract DPAPI (Data Protection API) master keys from memory or registry, which can be used to decrypt protected user data including saved passwords, certificates, and browser credentials. Attackers use tools like Mimikatz, SharpDPAPI, or custom PowerShell scripts to retrieve these keys and decrypt protected secrets.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1552.004 - Unsecured Credentials: Private Keys, T1140 - Deobfuscate/Decode Files
DetectionRule.ThreatActors: APT29, APT28, Lazarus, Ransomware Groups, Insider Threat
DetectionRule.AlertTitle: DPAPI Master Key Extraction Detected - Potential Credential Theft
DetectionRule.AlertDescription: Detected DPAPI master key extraction attempt on {DeviceName} by {AccountName}. This may indicate credential theft for lateral movement or data exfiltration.

RemediationSteps.Step: IMMEDIATE: Isolate affected device
Force password reset for all affected accounts
Revoke and reissue certificates and tokens
Enable Credential Guard on all Windows 10/11 devices
Audit DPAPI backup locations (domain controllers)
Review PowerShell execution policies and logging

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude authorized security tools with proper certificates
Time-based: Focus on off-hours activity
Exclude known admin workstations during maintenance windows

DetectionRule.Name: Browser Cookie and Session Bulk Export
DetectionRule.Description: Detects bulk export of browser cookies, authentication tokens, and session data which can be used to hijack active user sessions across web applications. Attackers use browser extensions, PowerShell, or direct file access to steal session cookies from Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and other browsers, enabling account takeover without needing passwords.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1539 - Steal Web Session Cookie, T1555.003 - Credentials from Web Browsers
DetectionRule.ThreatActors: APT29, APT28, Lazarus, Ransomware Groups, RedLine Stealer
DetectionRule.AlertTitle: Browser Cookie/Session Bulk Export Detected - Active Session Hijacking Risk
DetectionRule.AlertDescription: Detected bulk export of browser cookies and session tokens on {DeviceName}. This indicates active session hijacking attempts, allowing attackers to access web applications without credentials.

RemediationSteps.Step: IMMEDIATE: Force logout for all active sessions across all applications
IMMEDIATE: Reset passwords for all affected accounts
Revoke and reissue all authentication tokens and API keys
Enable MFA on all web applications (enforce phishing-resistant MFA)
Isolate affected device and investigate scope of breach
Review browser extensions and disable unnecessary ones
Implement Conditional Access policies for risky sign-ins

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude authorized backup tools with proper access
Exclude browser sync operations during known maintenance
IT admin workstations during authorized investigations
